title: Fix Trailing Whitespaces in String Concatenation

statement.markdown: Given a string array `nums`, write a function to concatenate all the strings in the array and remove any trailing whitespaces. For example, if the input is `['Hello', ' ', 'World', '  ']`, the output should be `'Hello World'`. The input will not contain any empty strings.
statement.outputFormat: Output is the concatenated string without trailing whitespaces.
statement.notes: Do not include any leading or trailing whitespaces in the output.

hints.text: Use a StringBuilder or string join operation to concatenate all the strings in the array.

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The idea is to use a StringBuilder or string join operation to concatenate all the strings in the array.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to use a loop to concatenate all the strings in the array.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a StringBuilder or string join operation to concatenate all the strings in the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a StringBuilder or string join operation to concatenate all the strings in the array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the properties of StringBuilder or string join operation.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run is provided in the solutions section.

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
